prompt: Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. TWO REFERENCE IMAGES are provided. The FIRST reference (navy variant) is the canonical TYPOGRAPHY ANCHOR — copy from it exactly: the product-name typeface, font weight, letter shapes, letter spacing, and overall label layout. The SECOND reference is the CAP COLOR ANCHOR — match its royal purple cap color and bottom accent bar color exactly. Reproduce vial geometry, lighting, and shadow from either reference. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #8068A8, do not drift. Vial size: 5ml. The label has the following content laid out top to bottom: (1) Upper roughly 60 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone with absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks. LOCKED TYPEFACE FOR ALL TEXT: Helvetica Neue. (2) Below the logo zone, the product name 'GLP-2 (T)' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (~700 weight) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered on the label, natural letter proportions. (3) Directly below, the dose-and-volume line '30 mg · 5 ml' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD at ~60 percent of product-name size, charcoal grey, perfectly centered. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, the compliance text rendered as TWO STACKED LINES, each line perfectly horizontally centered, in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300 weight, NOT bold), all-caps, in medium grey, at roughly 22 percent of the product-name size. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (5) Directly below with a small gap, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (NOT bold), perfectly horizontally centered with EQUAL margins on both sides — never left-aligned. (6) At the very bottom edge, the slim solid accent bar at exactly hex #8068A8 running edge to edge. NO hairlines anywhere. Sharp focus, high resolution, premium pharmaceutical product shot.
